entries.title: This psychology hack changes how you see people: Mirroring
entries.hook: This psychology hack changes how you see people:
explanations: People unconsciously copy body language of people they like. Try subtly mirroring someone they will feel connected to you.
If someone agrees to a small request they are much more likely to agree to a bigger one later.
Highly competent people become more likable when they make a small mistake. Perfection is off-putting.
Adding a third less attractive option makes one of the original two seem much better. That's why medium popcorn seems like the best deal.

entries.title: Psychology says: Loss aversion
entries.hook: Psychology says:
explanations: Losing $10 hurts twice as much as finding $10 feels good. Your brain is wired to avoid loss more than seek gain.
If someone is attractive your brain assumes they are also smart and kind. One positive trait colors everything.
Too many options make us unhappy. The brain prefers 3 choices over 30. Less is literally more.
Highly competent people become more likable when they make a small mistake. Perfection is off-putting.

entries.title: Psychology says: The spotlight effect
entries.hook: Psychology says:
explanations: You think everyone notices your mistakes. They don't. People are too busy thinking about themselves.
If someone does you a favor they will like you more not less. The brain justifies helping by assuming they like you.
If someone agrees to a small request they are much more likely to agree to a bigger one later.
Expecting more from someone makes them perform better. High expectations create high results.

entries.title: Your mind is more powerful than you think: The halo effect
entries.hook: Your mind is more powerful than you think:
explanations: If someone is attractive your brain assumes they are also smart and kind. One positive trait colors everything.
Your brain remembers unfinished tasks better than completed ones. That's why cliffhangers are so effective.
When someone gives you something your brain feels an overwhelming urge to give back. It's automatic.
Adding a third less attractive option makes one of the original two seem much better. That's why medium popcorn seems like the best deal.

entries.title: Your brain is playing tricks on you: The pratfall effect
entries.hook: Your brain is playing tricks on you:
explanations: Highly competent people become more likable when they make a small mistake. Perfection is off-putting.
Incompetent people overestimate their skills. Experts underestimate theirs. The more you know the less confident you feel.
People remember the first and last items in a list but forget the middle. First impressions and final moments matter most.
Adding a third less attractive option makes one of the original two seem much better. That's why medium popcorn seems like the best deal.

Adding a third less attractive option makes one of the original two seem much better. That's why medium popcorn seems like the best deal.

entries.title: Psychology says: The ben franklin effect
entries.hook: Psychology says:
explanations: If someone does you a favor they will like you more not less. The brain justifies helping by assuming they like you.
The more people who witness an emergency the less likely any individual is to help. Everyone assumes someone else will act.
Adding a third less attractive option makes one of the original two seem much better. That's why medium popcorn seems like the best deal.
People judge an experience based on how it felt at its peak and at its end not the total sum of moments.

entries.title: Psychology says: Mirroring
entries.hook: Psychology says:
explanations: People unconsciously copy body language of people they like. Try subtly mirroring someone they will feel connected to you.
Too many options make us unhappy. The brain prefers 3 choices over 30. Less is literally more.
Your brain remembers unfinished tasks better than completed ones. That's why cliffhangers are so effective.
Expecting more from someone makes them perform better. High expectations create high results.

entries.title: Most people don't know this about their brain: The ben franklin effect
entries.hook: Most people don't know this about their brain:
explanations: If someone does you a favor they will like you more not less. The brain justifies helping by assuming they like you.
Your brain remembers unfinished tasks better than completed ones. That's why cliffhangers are so effective.
The more people who witness an emergency the less likely any individual is to help. Everyone assumes someone else will act.
Adding a third less attractive option makes one of the original two seem much better. That's why medium popcorn seems like the best deal.

entries.title: Your brain is playing tricks on you: The spotlight effect
entries.hook: Your brain is playing tricks on you:
explanations: You think everyone notices your mistakes. They don't. People are too busy thinking about themselves.
Highly competent people become more likable when they make a small mistake. Perfection is off-putting.
Expecting more from someone makes them perform better. High expectations create high results.
Incompetent people overestimate their skills. Experts underestimate theirs. The more you know the less confident you feel.

entries.title: Most people don't know this about their brain: Loss aversion
entries.hook: Most people don't know this about their brain:
explanations: Losing $10 hurts twice as much as finding $10 feels good. Your brain is wired to avoid loss more than seek gain.
Too many options make us unhappy. The brain prefers 3 choices over 30. Less is literally more.
The first number you hear sets a mental anchor. That's why $99 feels much cheaper after seeing $199 first.
People remember the first and last items in a list but forget the middle. First impressions and final moments matter most.

entries.title: Psychology says: The ikea effect
entries.hook: Psychology says:
explanations: You value things more when you build them yourself. That is why DIY projects feel so satisfying.
If someone is attractive your brain assumes they are also smart and kind. One positive trait colors everything.
If someone agrees to a small request they are much more likely to agree to a bigger one later.
The more people who witness an emergency the less likely any individual is to help. Everyone assumes someone else will act.

entries.title: Psychology says: Cognitive dissonance
entries.hook: Psychology says:
explanations: When your actions don't match your beliefs your brain changes the belief not the behavior.
Highly competent people become more likable when they make a small mistake. Perfection is off-putting.
Your brain can heal you just by believing you received treatment. Fake pills work because your mind believes they will.
People judge an experience based on how it felt at its peak and at its end not the total sum of moments.

entries.title: Your mind is more powerful than you think: Choice paradox
entries.hook: Your mind is more powerful than you think:
explanations: Too many options make us unhappy. The brain prefers 3 choices over 30. Less is literally more.
Your brain remembers unfinished tasks better than completed ones. That's why cliffhangers are so effective.
The first number you hear sets a mental anchor. That's why $99 feels much cheaper after seeing $199 first.
Expecting more from someone makes them perform better. High expectations create high results.

entries.title: Your mind is more powerful than you think: The ben franklin effect
entries.hook: Your mind is more powerful than you think:
explanations: If someone does you a favor they will like you more not less. The brain justifies helping by assuming they like you.
People unconsciously copy body language of people they like. Try subtly mirroring someone they will feel connected to you.
If someone agrees to a small request they are much more likely to agree to a bigger one later.
When your actions don't match your beliefs your brain changes the belief not the behavior.

explanations: You value things more when you build them yourself. That is why DIY projects feel so satisfying.

Highly competent people become more likable when they make a small mistake. Perfection is off-putting.
Your brain can heal you just by believing you received treatment. Fake pills work because your mind believes they will.
People judge an experience based on how it felt at its peak and at its end not the total sum of moments.

entries.title: Your brain is playing tricks on you: The spotlight effect
entries.hook: Your brain is playing tricks on you:
explanations: You think everyone notices your mistakes. They don't. People are too busy thinking about themselves.
The first number you hear sets a mental anchor. That's why $99 feels much cheaper after seeing $199 first.
When someone gives you something your brain feels an overwhelming urge to give back. It's automatic.
People judge an experience based on how it felt at its peak and at its end not the total sum of moments.

entries.title: Psychology says: Foot-in-the-door
entries.hook: Psychology says:
explanations: If someone agrees to a small request they are much more likely to agree to a bigger one later.
Your brain remembers unfinished tasks better than completed ones. That's why cliffhangers are so effective.
Expecting more from someone makes them perform better. High expectations create high results.
People remember the first and last items in a list but forget the middle. First impressions and final moments matter most.

People unconsciously copy body language of people they like. Try subtly mirroring someone they will feel connected to you.
Your brain remembers unfinished tasks better than completed ones. That's why cliffhangers are so effective.
People remember the first and last items in a list but forget the middle. First impressions and final moments matter most.

entries.title: Your mind is more powerful than you think: The spotlight effect
entries.hook: Your mind is more powerful than you think:
explanations: You think everyone notices your mistakes. They don't. People are too busy thinking about themselves.
You value things more when you build them yourself. That is why DIY projects feel so satisfying.
If someone is attractive your brain assumes they are also smart and kind. One positive trait colors everything.
Adding a third less attractive option makes one of the original two seem much better. That's why medium popcorn seems like the best deal.

entries.title: Psychology says: Loss aversion
entries.hook: Psychology says:
explanations: Losing $10 hurts twice as much as finding $10 feels good. Your brain is wired to avoid loss more than seek gain.
People unconsciously copy body language of people they like. Try subtly mirroring someone they will feel connected to you.
Incompetent people overestimate their skills. Experts underestimate theirs. The more you know the less confident you feel.
People remember the first and last items in a list but forget the middle. First impressions and final moments matter most.

entries.title: Your mind is more powerful than you think: The spotlight effect
entries.hook: Your mind is more powerful than you think:
explanations: You think everyone notices your mistakes. They don't. People are too busy thinking about themselves.
Too many options make us unhappy. The brain prefers 3 choices over 30. Less is literally more.
The more people who witness an emergency the less likely any individual is to help. Everyone assumes someone else will act.
Your brain can heal you just by believing you received treatment. Fake pills work because your mind believes they will.

entries.title: Your brain is playing tricks on you: The ben franklin effect
entries.hook: Your brain is playing tricks on you:
explanations: If someone does you a favor they will like you more not less. The brain justifies helping by assuming they like you.
If someone agrees to a small request they are much more likely to agree to a bigger one later.

The more people who witness an emergency the less likely any individual is to help. Everyone assumes someone else will act.

entries.title: Most people don't know this about their brain: Choice paradox
entries.hook: Most people don't know this about their brain:
explanations: Too many options make us unhappy. The brain prefers 3 choices over 30. Less is literally more.
When your actions don't match your beliefs your brain changes the belief not the behavior.
Expecting more from someone makes them perform better. High expectations create high results.
People remember the first and last items in a list but forget the middle. First impressions and final moments matter most.

explanations: Your brain remembers unfinished tasks better than completed ones. That's why cliffhangers are so effective.
When your actions don't match your beliefs your brain changes the belief not the behavior.

explanations: If someone does you a favor they will like you more not less. The brain justifies helping by assuming they like you.
Too many options make us unhappy. The brain prefers 3 choices over 30. Less is literally more.
People remember the first and last items in a list but forget the middle. First impressions and final moments matter most.
The more people who witness an emergency the less likely any individual is to help. Everyone assumes someone else will act.

entries.title: Your mind is more powerful than you think: Choice paradox
entries.hook: Your mind is more powerful than you think:
explanations: Too many options make us unhappy. The brain prefers 3 choices over 30. Less is literally more.
Your brain remembers unfinished tasks better than completed ones. That's why cliffhangers are so effective.
The first number you hear sets a mental anchor. That's why $99 feels much cheaper after seeing $199 first.
When someone gives you something your brain feels an overwhelming urge to give back. It's automatic.

entries.title: Most people don't know this about their brain: Foot-in-the-door

explanations: If someone agrees to a small request they are much more likely to agree to a bigger one later.
When your actions don't match your beliefs your brain changes the belief not the behavior.

Your brain can heal you just by believing you received treatment. Fake pills work because your mind believes they will.

entries.title: Most people don't know this about their brain: Mirroring
entries.hook: Most people don't know this about their brain:
explanations: People unconsciously copy body language of people they like. Try subtly mirroring someone they will feel connected to you.
If someone is attractive your brain assumes they are also smart and kind. One positive trait colors everything.
Adding a third less attractive option makes one of the original two seem much better. That's why medium popcorn seems like the best deal.
People judge an experience based on how it felt at its peak and at its end not the total sum of moments.

entries.title: Your brain is playing tricks on you: Loss aversion
entries.hook: Your brain is playing tricks on you:
explanations: Losing $10 hurts twice as much as finding $10 feels good. Your brain is wired to avoid loss more than seek gain.
Your brain remembers unfinished tasks better than completed ones. That's why cliffhangers are so effective.
When your actions don't match your beliefs your brain changes the belief not the behavior.
Expecting more from someone makes them perform better. High expectations create high results.

entries.title: Your brain is playing tricks on you: Anchoring
entries.hook: Your brain is playing tricks on you:
explanations: The first number you hear sets a mental anchor. That's why $99 feels much cheaper after seeing $199 first.
Expecting more from someone makes them perform better. High expectations create high results.
The more people who witness an emergency the less likely any individual is to help. Everyone assumes someone else will act.
People judge an experience based on how it felt at its peak and at its end not the total sum of moments.

entries.title: Psychology says: Choice paradox
entries.hook: Psychology says:
explanations: Too many options make us unhappy. The brain prefers 3 choices over 30. Less is literally more.
Your brain remembers unfinished tasks better than completed ones. That's why cliffhangers are so effective.
The more people who witness an emergency the less likely any individual is to help. Everyone assumes someone else will act.
Your brain can heal you just by believing you received treatment. Fake pills work because your mind believes they will.

entries.title: Most people don't know this about their brain: The ikea effect
entries.hook: Most people don't know this about their brain:
explanations: You value things more when you build them yourself. That is why DIY projects feel so satisfying.
If someone is attractive your brain assumes they are also smart and kind. One positive trait colors everything.
When someone gives you something your brain feels an overwhelming urge to give back. It's automatic.
Adding a third less attractive option makes one of the original two seem much better. That's why medium popcorn seems like the best deal.

entries.title: Psychology says: Loss aversion
entries.hook: Psychology says:
explanations: Losing $10 hurts twice as much as finding $10 feels good. Your brain is wired to avoid loss more than seek gain.
Highly competent people become more likable when they make a small mistake. Perfection is off-putting.
People remember the first and last items in a list but forget the middle. First impressions and final moments matter most.
Adding a third less attractive option makes one of the original two seem much better. That's why medium popcorn seems like the best deal.

entries.title: Psychology says: The spotlight effect

explanations: You think everyone notices your mistakes. They don't. People are too busy thinking about themselves.
When your actions don't match your beliefs your brain changes the belief not the behavior.
Your brain can heal you just by believing you received treatment. Fake pills work because your mind believes they will.
People judge an experience based on how it felt at its peak and at its end not the total sum of moments.

entries.title: Most people don't know this about their brain: Choice paradox
entries.hook: Most people don't know this about their brain:
explanations: Too many options make us unhappy. The brain prefers 3 choices over 30. Less is literally more.
The first number you hear sets a mental anchor. That's why $99 feels much cheaper after seeing $199 first.

Adding a third less attractive option makes one of the original two seem much better. That's why medium popcorn seems like the best deal.

entries.title: This psychology hack changes how you see people: The zeigarnik effect
entries.hook: This psychology hack changes how you see people:
explanations: Your brain remembers unfinished tasks better than completed ones. That's why cliffhangers are so effective.
The first number you hear sets a mental anchor. That's why $99 feels much cheaper after seeing $199 first.
Expecting more from someone makes them perform better. High expectations create high results.
The more people who witness an emergency the less likely any individual is to help. Everyone assumes someone else will act.

entries.title: Psychology says: The spotlight effect
entries.hook: Psychology says:
explanations: You think everyone notices your mistakes. They don't. People are too busy thinking about themselves.
Losing $10 hurts twice as much as finding $10 feels good. Your brain is wired to avoid loss more than seek gain.
People unconsciously copy body language of people they like. Try subtly mirroring someone they will feel connected to you.
Too many options make us unhappy. The brain prefers 3 choices over 30. Less is literally more.

entries.title: This psychology hack changes how you see people: Loss aversion
entries.hook: This psychology hack changes how you see people:
explanations: Losing $10 hurts twice as much as finding $10 feels good. Your brain is wired to avoid loss more than seek gain.
If someone is attractive your brain assumes they are also smart and kind. One positive trait colors everything.
The first number you hear sets a mental anchor. That's why $99 feels much cheaper after seeing $199 first.
Adding a third less attractive option makes one of the original two seem much better. That's why medium popcorn seems like the best deal.

entries.title: Psychology says: The spotlight effect
entries.hook: Psychology says:
explanations: You think everyone notices your mistakes. They don't. People are too busy thinking about themselves.
If someone agrees to a small request they are much more likely to agree to a bigger one later.
Highly competent people become more likable when they make a small mistake. Perfection is off-putting.
Adding a third less attractive option makes one of the original two seem much better. That's why medium popcorn seems like the best deal.

entries.title: Your mind is more powerful than you think: The ben franklin effect
entries.hook: Your mind is more powerful than you think:
explanations: If someone does you a favor they will like you more not less. The brain justifies helping by assuming they like you.
Too many options make us unhappy. The brain prefers 3 choices over 30. Less is literally more.
When someone gives you something your brain feels an overwhelming urge to give back. It's automatic.
The more people who witness an emergency the less likely any individual is to help. Everyone assumes someone else will act.

Adding a third less attractive option makes one of the original two seem much better. That's why medium popcorn seems like the best deal.

explanations: You think everyone notices your mistakes. They don't. People are too busy thinking about themselves.
You value things more when you build them yourself. That is why DIY projects feel so satisfying.
Too many options make us unhappy. The brain prefers 3 choices over 30. Less is literally more.
People remember the first and last items in a list but forget the middle. First impressions and final moments matter most.

entries.title: Your mind is more powerful than you think: The zeigarnik effect
entries.hook: Your mind is more powerful than you think:
explanations: Your brain remembers unfinished tasks better than completed ones. That's why cliffhangers are so effective.
The first number you hear sets a mental anchor. That's why $99 feels much cheaper after seeing $199 first.
Incompetent people overestimate their skills. Experts underestimate theirs. The more you know the less confident you feel.
The more people who witness an emergency the less likely any individual is to help. Everyone assumes someone else will act.

entries.title: Your brain is playing tricks on you: The halo effect
entries.hook: Your brain is playing tricks on you:
explanations: If someone is attractive your brain assumes they are also smart and kind. One positive trait colors everything.
Too many options make us unhappy. The brain prefers 3 choices over 30. Less is literally more.
When someone gives you something your brain feels an overwhelming urge to give back. It's automatic.
Incompetent people overestimate their skills. Experts underestimate theirs. The more you know the less confident you feel.

entries.title: Psychology says: Mirroring
entries.hook: Psychology says:
explanations: People unconsciously copy body language of people they like. Try subtly mirroring someone they will feel connected to you.
Too many options make us unhappy. The brain prefers 3 choices over 30. Less is literally more.
Expecting more from someone makes them perform better. High expectations create high results.
Adding a third less attractive option makes one of the original two seem much better. That's why medium popcorn seems like the best deal.

entries.title: Psychology says: The spotlight effect
entries.hook: Psychology says:
explanations: You think everyone notices your mistakes. They don't. People are too busy thinking about themselves.
If someone agrees to a small request they are much more likely to agree to a bigger one later.
Highly competent people become more likable when they make a small mistake. Perfection is off-putting.
When someone gives you something your brain feels an overwhelming urge to give back. It's automatic.

entries.title: Psychology says: The ben franklin effect
entries.hook: Psychology says:
explanations: If someone does you a favor they will like you more not less. The brain justifies helping by assuming they like you.
When your actions don't match your beliefs your brain changes the belief not the behavior.
Highly competent people become more likable when they make a small mistake. Perfection is off-putting.
Adding a third less attractive option makes one of the original two seem much better. That's why medium popcorn seems like the best deal.

entries.title: This psychology hack changes how you see people: Loss aversion
entries.hook: This psychology hack changes how you see people:
explanations: Losing $10 hurts twice as much as finding $10 feels good. Your brain is wired to avoid loss more than seek gain.
Too many options make us unhappy. The brain prefers 3 choices over 30. Less is literally more.
Highly competent people become more likable when they make a small mistake. Perfection is off-putting.
The more people who witness an emergency the less likely any individual is to help. Everyone assumes someone else will act.
